frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing FLS trainer box with gauze pad and Penrose drain in position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean setup, drain properly positioned on gauze

frame_analyses.description: Clear view of Penrose drain with visible slit and marking dots for suture placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good visualization of target marks on drain

frame_analyses.description: Right hand needle holder grasping suture, beginning needle loading process
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument grip, suture appears to be 2-0 silk

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments working together, suture being passed through first mark on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bimanual coordination for suture placement

frame_analyses.description: Suture passing through drain, both marks appear to have been successfully traversed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Accurate suture placement through both target marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot with right hand manipulation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initiating double throw technique for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Completing surgeon's knot double throw, maintaining tension
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tension control during knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Switching hands for second throw, left hand now active
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching technique between throws

frame_analyses.description: Switching hands again for third throw, right hand back to active position
Final knot tightened, drain slit appears closed, ready for cutting
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure, good slit closure achieved

frame_analyses.description: Task completed, knot secured and drain properly closed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final result shows secure knot with good tissue approximation

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw technique
Single throw with proper hand switch from previous throw
Final single throw with proper hand switch, knot appears secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Excellent slit closure with no visible gap, drain remains intact

technique_summary: Excellent performance with proper surgeon's knot technique, correct hand switching between throws, and accurate suture placement. The trainee demonstrated good bimanual coordination and achieved complete slit closure.
improvement_suggestions: Consider optimizing needle loading time to reduce overall completion time
Practice maintaining consistent instrument angles during knot tying
strengths: Accurate suture placement at target marks
Proper surgeon's knot technique with double throw
Correct hand switching pattern between all throws
Secure knot with complete slit closure
